parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces substantial new infra (retry, bigmodel-errors, job tracking, CI gates) but the cumulative changes carry real risk: the new retry.mjs and glm-client.mjs interaction has unguarded recursion potential on persistent failures, tracked-jobs state can silently desync from actual BigModel job state, and the deleted process.mjs removes utilities other modules may still reference. Several critical paths lack observability for partial-failure diagnosis. Recommend hardening before calling this sweep-ready.

findings.title: retry.mjs retryWithBackoff can mask persistent failures silently
findings.body: The new retry.mjs module provides a general-purpose retryWithBackoff that is called from glm-client.mjs for BigModel API calls. If the BigModel API returns a steady stream of retryable errors (e.g. 429 rate-limit or transient 5xx), the retry loop will consume the entire retry budget and then throw — but any side effects from partially-completed request setup (e.g. job state written to tracked-jobs, temp files created) are NOT cleaned up on final failure. This creates orphan state that confuses subsequent status/poll commands. The absence of a finally/cleanup hook in the retry wrapper means every caller must independently handle teardown, and the glm-client.mjs caller does not.
findings.recommendation: Add an onFinalFailure or cleanup callback option to retryWithBackoff, and have glm-client.mjs use it to remove any tracked-job entries or temp state created before the retry sequence began. Alternatively, structure job tracking as a two-phase commit: only persist after first successful response.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs can desync from actual BigModel job lifecycle
findings.body: The tracked-jobs module manages local state about submitted BigModel jobs, but there is no heartbeat or reconciliation mechanism. If the local process crashes or is killed between submitting a job and recording it in tracked-jobs (or between a poll and a state update), the local state diverges from reality. On restart, commands like /glm:status or the review --wait path will see stale/missing entries with no way to detect the inconsistency. The diff shows state.mjs changes and tracked-jobs.mjs changes but no recovery/reconciliation logic.
findings.recommendation: Add a reconciliation step: on startup/status, query BigModel for any jobs associated with the configured API key/project that are not in tracked-jobs, and either import or flag them. At minimum, add a staleness timestamp and warn when tracked entries have not been updated in N minutes.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs error classification may misclassify unknown error shapes
findings.body: The new bigmodel-errors.mjs module classifies API errors to determine retryability and dispatch behavior. If BigModel changes its error response schema (or returns an unexpected shape — e.g. a network-level error that is not a proper JSON body), the classifier will fall through to a default that may be retry=true or retry=false depending on the implementation. A wrong classification on a non-idempotent write operation (like job submission) could cause duplicate submissions or missed retries. The test file exists but coverage of edge cases (non-JSON responses, truncated responses, HTML error pages from proxies) is not evidenced.
findings.recommendation: Audit the default/fallback classification in classifyError to ensure unknown errors default to non-retryable for mutating operations. Add explicit tests for: non-JSON response bodies, network timeouts, DNS failures, and proxy HTML error pages. Document the contract: what does 'unknown' mean for retry decisions?

findings.title: glm-client.mjs retry integration may cause duplicate job submissions on timeout
findings.body: glm-client.mjs wraps BigModel API calls with retry logic. For job-submission endpoints, a timeout after the request was received but before the response was read means the job was created server-side but the client sees a failure. On retry, a second job is submitted. This is the classic non-idempotent-retry problem. Without an idempotency key or deduplication mechanism, users can get duplicate jobs running in parallel, consuming quota and producing duplicate review output.
findings.recommendation: For job-submission calls, either: (a) generate and send an idempotency key that BigModel can use to deduplicate, or (b) on retry after timeout, first poll for any recently-created job matching the same parameters before re-submitting. At minimum, document this as a known limitation and add a warning in logs when retrying a POST/submit operation.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted but may still be imported by external consumers or plugin hooks
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines removed) was deleted in this diff. If any external tooling, hooks, or user scripts import from this module (directly or via re-export), they will encounter a runtime ModuleNotFoundError at invocation time. The session-lifecycle-hook.mjs was modified, but there is no evidence of a deprecation period or migration guide for downstream consumers.
findings.recommendation: Verify no external consumers reference process.mjs (search npm registry, local scripts, hooks). If any exist, restore the module with a deprecation stub that logs a warning and re-exports from the replacement module, rather than hard-deleting.

findings.title: CI check-no-local-paths.sh may false-negative on encoded paths or multi-line strings
findings.body: The new CI guard check-no-local-paths.sh scans for hardcoded local filesystem paths in committed files. Simple regex-based approaches can miss: URL-encoded paths, paths split across line boundaries, or paths inside binary/marshaled data. A false negative here would leak developer machine paths into the release, which is a security/reputation concern given the stated purpose of this check.
findings.recommendation: Supplement the regex check with a known-bad-prefix list (e.g. /home/, /Users/, C:\) and test against actual leak cases from prior incidents. Consider adding the check as a pre-commit hook for immediate feedback rather than CI-only.

next_steps: Read scripts/lib/retry.mjs and scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs in full to confirm the retry-without-cleanup and duplicate-submission hypotheses; trace the exact call chain for job submission with retries.
Read scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs and scripts/lib/state.mjs to verify whether any reconciliation/staleness-detection exists post-crash.
Read scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs to audit the default classification for unknown error shapes.
Search codebase for any remaining imports of scripts/lib/process.mjs to confirm the deletion is safe.
Run the existing test suite (tests/retry.test.mjs, tests/bigmodel-errors.test.mjs) and verify coverage of timeout-during-submit and non-JSON-response edge cases.
